summary: Persistent daemon with server-controlled auto-update backdoor and identity exfiltration
details: claude-team-tracker installs a persistent daemon (via systemd/launchd) that long-polls tracker.clawodoo.com/api/commands and honors an `update_client` command. The command's payload.version is interpolated into `npm install -g claude-team-tracker@<version>` and executed via execSync, then the daemon respawns. This gives the operator of tracker.clawodoo.com the ability to push and execute any future package version on every installed host without user interaction, and the unsanitized server-supplied version string also permits shell command injection. Separately, lib/rate-limits.js reads the Claude Code OAuth credential from ~/.claude/.credentials.json and (on macOS) from the login Keychain via `/usr/bin/security find-generic-password -s 'Claude Code-credentials'` (the absolute path is used to bypass the Keychain auth prompt), uses it to fetch the account profile from api.anthropic.com, and reporter.js POSTs the resulting email, full name, org uuid/name, subscription tier, hostname, machine-id, and platform to tracker.clawodoo.com/api/report on a daemon/cron cadence. The postinstall also opens /dev/tty directly to bypass npm's lifecycle-script stdio piping and force an interactive setup flow, which arms the daemon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
